conflict: Burt's fall threatens exposure and forces the ring to confront the fact that its members are aging and fragile
consequence: Carmen conceals physical evidence and calls herself part of 'us' for the first time; Burt's decline shows biology is the ring's real adversary
must_land_beats: Burt's fall at 3 AM — scalp laceration, Earl pressing the call bell
Carmen realizes the pill stash is hidden in Burt's room
Carmen takes the Hungarian-English dictionary and hides it — active evidence tampering
Burt returns from hospital more diminished
Ring debates Burt's continued participation: Harry wants to continue, Agnes argues against, Earl is silent
Carmen says 'He'd want us to keep going' — startled by her own 'us'

payoffs_to_land.description: The Hungarian-English dictionary from Burt's wife, established as meaningful in Ch 3, is the pill stash hiding place Carmen must grab before EMTs arrive

pressure_source: Burt's fall threatens exposure and forces the ring to confront the fact that its members are aging and fragile
secondary_character_beats: Earl pressing the call bell for his roommate — genuine care, not just operational concern
Agnes's opposition to using Burt's prescriptions without meaningful consent is a pharmacist's ethical reflex, not sentimentality
state_shift: Carmen conceals physical evidence and calls herself part of 'us' for the first time; Burt's decline shows biology is the ring's real adversary
